pytorch.reason: load_bearing: Werner density matrix construction, von Neumann entropy via torch.linalg.eigvalsh, I_c computation, node feature tensor for MessagePassing

pyg.reason: load_bearing: dynamic-edge Werner graph; edge_attr = I_c(p_i, p_j) if both I_c>0 else 0; WernerMessagePassing aggregates I_c-weighted features from quantum neighbors

z3.reason: load_bearing: UNSAT proof that Werner state with p > p_sep=1/3 (separable) cannot have I_c > 0; encodes analytic separability boundary

cvc5.reason: not needed — z3 UNSAT proof is sufficient

sympy.reason: not needed — analytic results carried from werner_manifold_scan

e3nn.reason: not needed — no SO(3) equivariance required

rustworkx.reason: load_bearing: directed DAG of quantum channels (edge iff I_c > 0); topological sort identifies ordering; betweenness centrality finds quantum bottleneck

xgi.reason: load_bearing: 3-regime hyperedge over Werner nodes: regime_positive (I_c>0), regime_entangled (I_c<=0 but p<1/3), regime_separable (p>=1/3); intersection check identifies boundary nodes

toponetx.reason: not needed — hyperedge structure handled by xgi

positive.bifurcation_note: deep_pos (p=0.1053, ic=0.3302) aggregation norm=1.12565 (nonzero: has quantum neighbors). boundary (p=0.2632, ic=-0.020417) aggregation norm=0.0 (isolated: I_c<=0, no quantum neighbors). deep_neg (p=0.4211) aggregation norm=0.0 (isolated: I_c<=0). Bifurcation at p_I_c_zero~0.252 is visible as a sharp drop from nonzero to zero aggregation norm when crossing from I_c>0 to I_c<=0 regime.

boundary.z3_proof_strategy: Encoded: p >= 1/3 AND I_c <= I_c(1/3) AND I_c > 0. I_c(1/3) = -0.14384104 < 0 (numerically certified). This is UNSAT because no real number can be both <= negative_value and > 0.
